context: Tests that the agent detects hardcoded HTTP_Passwd in the Elasticsearch OUTPUT, hardcoded AWS credentials in the S3 OUTPUT, and tls.verify Off in the Elasticsearch OUTPUT, then produces a corrected config using ${ENV_VAR} references and restores TLS verification.

checklist.name: Hardcoded HTTP_Passwd detected
checklist.description: security-report.md (or equivalent) flags the plaintext password in the Elasticsearch OUTPUT HTTP_Passwd field as a hardcoded credential

checklist.name: Hardcoded AWS credentials detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags both AWS_Access_Key_ID and AWS_Secret_Access_Key in the S3 OUTPUT as hardcoded credentials

checklist.name: tls.verify Off detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags `tls.verify Off` in the Elasticsearch OUTPUT as a TLS certificate verification bypass

checklist.name: HTTP_Passwd replaced with env var reference
checklist.description: In the corrected fluent-bit.conf, HTTP_Passwd uses a ${ENV_VAR} reference (e.g., ${ES_PASSWORD}) instead of the plaintext value

checklist.name: AWS credentials replaced with env var references
checklist.description: In the corrected fluent-bit.conf, AWS_Access_Key_ID and AWS_Secret_Access_Key use ${ENV_VAR} references instead of literal values

checklist.name: tls.verify corrected
checklist.description: In the corrected fluent-bit.conf, tls.verify is set to On in the Elasticsearch OUTPUT

checklist.name: Severity assigned to each finding
checklist.description: security-report.md assigns a severity (HIGH, MEDIUM, or INFO) to each finding

checklist.name: Risk explanation for each finding
checklist.description: security-report.md provides a brief explanation of the risk for each finding (e.g., credential exposure in version control, MITM vulnerability)
